message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Implementing deep-merge behavior in Features.update()

type: CLAIM
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Adding keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class to sync _column_requires_decoding dict on mutation

type: QUESTION
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: I'm replacing manual __setitem__/__delitem__/update overrides with a keep_features_dicts_synced decorator (wraps dict.update etc, recomputes _column_requires_decoding after each op). This may conflict with your update() deep-merge change.
blocked_on: need to know if your update() deep-merge logic must run key-by-key (to trigger custom __setitem__) or can be a final recompute of _column_requires_decoding after dict.update() runs

type: ANSWER
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: My update() fully replaces the body: no super()/dict.update() call. It iterates key-by-key via a new _merge_dict_value(key, value) helper that either deep-merges in place (recursing via a module-level _deep_merge_dicts helper) or assigns directly, and always finishes with self[key] = <merged-or-new value> so __setitem__ still fires per key. So your decorator can wrap __setitem__ (fires per key as before) or wrap update() as a whole (safe since a full recompute after is idempotent) - either works with my code. I did not touch __setitem__/__delitem__ themselves.

type: STATUS
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Deep-merge Features.update() implemented and tested (added _deep_merge_dicts helper + _merge_dict_value; update() still key-by-key via self[key]=... so __setitem__ fires normally); patch.txt written.

type: STATUS
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Kept update = keep_features_dicts_synced(dict.update) in my patch (shallow merge, same semantics as old hand-written update()) since my task must stand alone; when integrating our patches you'll need to replace this class-level 'update = ...' line with your full deep-merge method body (still calling self[key]=... so it hits my synced __setitem__, or otherwise recompute _column_requires_decoding via keep_features_dicts_synced at the end of your method).
